SUMMARY AND OUTCOME The Fiscal Year (FY) 2023-2024 Affordable Housing Impact Fee (AHIF) and Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Annual Report outlines the collection, disbursement, and planned use of fees to fund the creation of affordable housing, as required by the California Mitigation Fee Act.1 During FY 2023-2024, no new fees for the AHIF program were collected, although one residential development repaid the City $27,213.38 in interest on an AHIF-funded loan from a previous fiscal year. Currently, the City’s AHIF fund has $5,023,101 encumbered for the construction of the Parkmoor Community Apartments, which will offer 81 units, of which 79 will be restricted affordable units for extremely low-, very low-, and low-income renters and two will be unrestricted managers’ apartments. During FY 2023-2024, City staff collected $4,915,231.56 in CLF revenues. These revenues comprised $4,893,301.56 in linkage fees collected from six developments, $23,595 in application fees collected from 21 developments, and $1,665 in refund transactions disbursed to three developers. The CLF revenue funds were carried into FY 2024-2025 as part of the Unrestricted Ending Fund Reserves. 1 California Government Code Section 66000 et seq. COUNCIL AGENDA: 12/10/24 FILE: 24-2287 ITEM: 2.9 HONORABLE MAYOR AND CITY COUNCIL November 19, 2024 Subject: Fiscal Year 2023-2024 Affordable Housing Impact Fee and Commercial Linkage Fee Annual Report Page 2 BACKGROUND The California Mitigation Fee Act The California Mitigation Fee Act requires public agencies to account for and make specific findings regarding mitigation fees collected by an agency as a condition of development approval. 2 The Mitigation Fee Act regulates how public agencies establish, collect, maintain, and spend impact fees imposed on develo [normalized=27213.38 ; unit=usd_per_square_foot ; source_locator=attachment_probe:excerpt ; locator_quality=attachment_probe_excerpt ; source_ref=legistar::attachment::33720 ; source_family=memorandum ; attachment_id=33720 ; confidence=0.70]

SUMMARY AND OUTCOME The Fiscal Year (FY) 2023-2024 Affordable Housing Impact Fee (AHIF) and Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Annual Report outlines the collection, disbursement, and planned use of fees to fund the creation of affordable housing, as required by the California Mitigation Fee Act.1 During FY 2023-2024, no new fees for the AHIF program were collected, although one residential development repaid the City $27,213.38 in interest on an AHIF-funded loan from a previous fiscal year. Currently, the City’s AHIF fund has $5,023,101 encumbered for the construction of the Parkmoor Community Apartments, which will offer 81 units, of which 79 will be restricted affordable units for extremely low-, very low-, and low-income renters and two will be unrestricted managers’ apartments. During FY 2023-2024, City staff collected $4,915,231.56 in CLF revenues. These revenues comprised $4,893,301.56 in linkage fees collected from six developments, $23,595 in application fees collected from 21 developments, and $1,665 in refund transactions disbursed to three developers. The CLF revenue funds were carried into FY 2024-2025 as part of the Unrestricted Ending Fund Reserves. 1 California Government Code Section 66000 et seq. SUMMARY AND OUTCOME The Fiscal Year (FY) 2023-2024 Affordable Housing Impact Fee (AHIF) and Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Annual Report outlines the collection, disbursement, and planned use of fees to fund the creation of affordable housing, as required by the California Mitigation Fee Act.1 During FY 2023-2024, no new fees for the AHIF program were collected, although one residential development repaid the City $27,213.38 in interest on an AHIF-funded loan from a previous fiscal year. Currently, the City’s AHIF fund has $5,023,101 encumbered for the construction of the Parkmoor Community Apartments, which will offer 81 units, of which 79 will be restricted affordable units for extremely low-, very low-, and low-income renters and two will be unrestricted managers’ apartments. During FY 2023-2024, City staff collected $4,915,231.56 in CLF revenues. These revenues comprised $4,893,301.56 in linkage fees collected from six developments, $23,595 in application fees collected from 21 developments, and $1,665 in refund transactions disbursed to three developers. The CLF revenue funds were carried into FY 2024-2025 as part of the Unrestricted Ending Fund Reserves. 1 California Government Code Section 66000 et seq. SUMMARY AND OUTCOME The Fiscal Year (FY) 2023-2024 Affordable Housing Impact Fee (AHIF) and Commercial Linkage Fee (CLF) Annual Report outlines the collection, disbursement, and planned use of fees to fund the creation of affordable housing, as required by the California Mitigation Fee Act.1 During FY 2023-2024, no new fees for the AHIF program were collected, although one residential development repaid the City $27,213.38 in interest on an AHIF-funded loan from a previous fiscal year. Currently, the City’s AHIF fund has $5,023,101 encumbered for the construction of the Parkmoor Community Apartments, which will offer 81 units, of which 79 will be restricted affordable units for extremely low-, very low-, and low-income renters and two will be unrestricted managers’ apartments. During FY 2023-2024, City staff collected $4,915,231.56 in CLF revenues. These revenues comprised $4,893,301.56 in linkage fees collected from six developments, $23,595 in application fees collected from 21 developments, and $1,665 in refund transactions disbursed to three developers. The CLF revenue funds were carried into FY 2024-2025 as part of the Unrestricted Ending Fund Reserves. 1 California Government Code Section 66000 et seq.
